title: Cowpea Leaf Disk Collection Protocol
author: Stephanie Cullum
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rspd6dn

steps.title: Also, if possible, set up program...
steps.instruction: Also, if possible, set up program to randomise the leaf disk collections onto the 9 by 6 leaf plate tray (if not then just randomly place them as best as you can during the collections).

steps.title: For the best quality results and...
steps.instruction: For the best quality results and to not inhibit plant growth, only take leaf disks from plants big enough to survive the damage.

steps.title: Leave the last column free as...
steps.instruction: Leave the last column free as this will be used as a plate number marker

steps.title: if not then do repeat disks on the same plant
steps.instruction: if not then do repeat disks on the same plant

steps.title: If the hole punch works correctly...
steps.instruction: If the hole punch works correctly then a second press of the hole punch removes the disk. However, the hole punch can be temperamental and the leaf disk may fall or stick to the punch or get stuck within the punch. The sharp pointed object can be used in such cases to free the leaf disk

steps.title: To place the disk so that...
steps.instruction: To place the disk so that it is flat, use the pointed object to manipulate the leaf into place. Make note of the placement of the leaf disk within the tray e.g. A6

steps.title: After filling the tray, place a...
steps.instruction: After filling the tray, place a leaf piece into thr right hand column top row to indicate that this tray is the 1st.
